evals.prompt: I use Fedora Bazzite/KDE and I want to stop being afraid of rebooting. Configure my desktop so Konsole/tmux, my browser tabs, and reopened apps recover as much as possible. First check what browser/app setup I actually use.
evals.expected_output: Uses the Fedora KDE bundled script or equivalent layered setup for KDE session restore, desired browser startup restore, Konsole tmux profile, tmux persistence, and user systemd service, while inspecting installed/desired apps and explaining realistic limits.

evals.prompt: Rename my old sustainable-terminal skill into a broader sustainable home system skill with scripts/templates instead of a giant prose-only SKILL.md.
evals.expected_output: Creates a sustainable-home-system skill, moves concrete configs into templates/scripts, updates metadata, and removes/replaces the terminal-only skill name.

evals.prompt: Explain what I can and cannot expect to restore after a hard reboot on Kinoite: browser tabs, terminal tabs, Discord, dev servers, editors, and random GUI windows.
evals.expected_output: Explains layered restore guarantees and limitations: KDE reopens windows where supported, configured browsers restore tabs, tmux restores terminal workspace snapshots, running processes die, app-specific state varies.
